ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry specific to Jollibee Chickenjoy spicy variant; closest parent 'fried chicken food product' (FOODON_03301521) used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Chicken meat, fried) is the closest defensible parent; no specific code for spicy fast-food fried chicken variant exists in FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 255620007 'Fried chicken (food)' is the closest parent; no code specific to Jollibee Chickenjoy spicy variant exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: One serving is a single piece of Jollibee Chickenjoy spicy fried chicken (drumstick or thigh), weighing approximately 120 g, as sold at Jollibee Singapore outlets.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken, broilers or fryers, drumstick, meat and skin, cooked, fried, batter (USDA SR Legacy, NDB 05062); adapted with spicy seasoning adjustment referencing USDA Branded Food entries for fast-food spicy fried chicken
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy and Branded Foods)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary nutrient base derived from USDA SR Legacy NDB 05062 (fried battered chicken drumstick, meat and skin) and cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy NDB 05064 (fried battered chicken thigh, meat and skin). Values represent a weighted average of drumstick and thigh portions at approximately 120 g per piece. Spicy seasoning adds negligible macronutrient change but may slightly elevate sodium; sodium value adjusted upward by approximately 10% to reflect spicy marinade. This is the best available approved-source approximation for Jollibee Chickenjoy spicy fried chicken, as no HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN entry exists for this specific branded product.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of fried chicken and battered chicken products — analogous estimate based on battered fried chicken (GI ~45) from University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson FS et al. (2008) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Jollibee Chickenjoy spicy fried chicken. GI of 45 is estimated from battered fried chicken entries in the University of Sydney GI Database and the International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283). Battered fried chicken has a low-to-medium GI due to the wheat flour batter coating; GI 45 (low category) is the defensible analogous estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 × 9.84) / 100 ≈ 4.4, where carbohydrate per 120 g serving = 8.2 × 1.2 = 9.84 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~490 mg per 100 g; ~588 mg per 120 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of Jollibee Chickenjoy spicy fried chicken due to its high sodium content (~588 mg per piece), which represents approximately 25% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2,000 mg; pairing with low-sodium sides and limiting frequency is advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate carbohydrate load from batter coating with low glycemic index (~45); high fat content may impair postprandial insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes can consume Jollibee Chickenjoy spicy fried chicken occasionally, as its low GI (~45) and modest carbohydrate content (~9.8 g per piece) produce a low glycemic load (~4.4), but the high total fat (15.8 g/100 g) and saturated fat content may impair insulin sensitivity and should be factored into overall dietary fat targets.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~4.2 g/100 g), trans fat (~0.1 g/100 g), cholesterol (~85 mg/100 g), and sodium (~490 mg/100 g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat Jollibee Chickenjoy spicy fried chicken as an occasional food only, given its combination of saturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, and high sodium, all of which are established risk factors for atherosclerosis and elevated blood pressure; frequency should be minimised and portion limited to one piece per occasion.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Jollibee Chickenjoy Spicy Fried Chicken (1 piece) is a single piece of deep-fried chicken — either a drumstick or thigh — sold by Jollibee, the Filipino fast-food chain with outlets across Singapore including Bugis Village. The chicken is marinated in a proprietary spicy blend of chilli and spices, coated in a seasoned wheat flour batter, and deep-fried in vegetable oil until the skin is crispy and golden. The interior meat is juicy and tender, while the exterior batter provides a crunchy, spicy coating with a mild to moderate heat level. Each piece weighs approximately 120 g and delivers around 312 kcal, 27 g of protein, 19 g of fat, and 9.8 g of carbohydrate. The sodium content is notable at approximately 588 mg per piece, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 45 (low category), producing a glycemic load of approximately 4.4 per serving, which is relatively modest for people with Type 2 Diabetes. Chickenjoy is a flagship product of Jollibee and is widely recognised across Southeast Asia and among Filipino diaspora communities in Singapore. It is certified Halal in Singapore. The spicy variant ('D' designation on field survey data) is distinguished from the classic variant by its chilli-forward marinade. Nutritionally, it is a high-protein, moderate-fat fast-food item best consumed in moderation as part of a balanced diet.

Is Jollibee Chickenjoy halal in Singapore?

Jollibee spicy chicken nutrition facts Singapore

confidence_reason: No exact branded entry for Jollibee Chickenjoy spicy fried chicken exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or USDA Branded Foods. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy battered fried chicken drumstick and thigh entries (NDB 05062, 05064), which are the closest approved-source analogues. Sodium adjusted upward by ~10% to account for spicy marinade. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database battered fried chicken data. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy NDB 05062 (fried battered chicken drumstick) and NDB 05064 (fried battered chicken thigh), weighted average for a ~120 g piece. No exact Jollibee Chickenjoy branded entry was found in any approved primary source. 2. Sodium is adjusted upward by ~10% from the USDA base value to reflect the spicy marinade formulation. 3. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries for fried battered chicken. 4. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries for fried battered chicken. 5. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database battered fried chicken data; no direct GI measurement for Jollibee Chickenjoy spicy exists in any approved GI source. 6. The food is tagged Halal on field survey data (Jollibee Singapore is Halal-certified by MUIS). 7. The '(D)' designation in the field survey listing refers to the drumstick portion option; however, Chickenjoy is also served as thigh; nutrient values represent a weighted average of both cuts. 8. trans_fat_g of 0.1 g/100 g reflects trace trans fats naturally present in chicken fat (vaccenic acid); this is not industrially added trans fat. 9. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0; confirmed zero per USDA SR Legacy — fried battered chicken contains no meaningful vitamin C.
